name: Big Nose
validation_source: ScubaBoard user Dan (GPS only, no site-specific sources found)

tags.name: Big Nose
tags.description: Dive site in the north Komodo area. Site details unverified — contributed from a diver's personal logbook. Located in open water northwest of Komodo Island. May be known by a different name among local operators.

name: Golden Passage
validation_source: divekomodo.com, lagaligoliveaboard.com, liveaboard.com, ScubaBoard user Dan (GPS)

tags.name: Golden Passage
tags.description: A high-energy drift dive through the narrow channel between Gili Lawa Darat and Komodo Island. Tidal currents reaching several knots sweep divers past volcanic rock walls covered in hard and soft corals. Schools of trevally, whitetip and blacktip reef sharks, hawksbill turtles, and occasional manta rays gather in the nutrient-rich flow.

name: Castle Rock
validation_source: notroublesjustbubbles.com, divekomodo.com, nerendivingkomodo.net, ScubaBoard user Dan (GPS)

tags.name: Castle Rock
tags.description: A large underwater seamount north of Gili Lawa Laut with its peak just meters below the surface. Blanketed in soft corals and sea fans hosting pygmy seahorses. Medium to very strong currents draw huge schools of fusiliers, whitetip and grey reef sharks, giant trevally, napoleon wrasse, and occasional eagle rays. Widely considered one of Komodo's best dive sites.

name: Batu Bolong
validation_source: diveconcepts.com, divekomodo.com, azulkomodo.com, ScubaBoard user Dan (GPS)

tags.name: Batu Bolong
tags.description: An exposed rock pinnacle rising from over 70m in the center of Lintah Strait. Strong currents hit alternating sides depending on tide. Every inch of the pinnacle is covered in healthy coral, hosting enormous densities of anthias, reef sharks, giant trevally, dogtooth tuna, and napoleon wrasse. Regularly ranked among the world's top dive sites.

tags.name: Secret Garden
tags.description: A sheltered bay reef off northeast Padar Island with mild currents. Features large green and orange black coral bushes, vibrant soft corals, and rich macro life including mantis shrimp, ribbon eels, blue-ringed octopus, ghost pipefish, and nudibranchs. A calm alternative to Komodo's current-swept northern sites.

tags.name: Padar Bay
tags.description: A protected bay on the south side of Padar offering sheltered reef diving with gentle slopes and coral gardens. Calm conditions make it ideal for less experienced divers and a relaxing complement to the more demanding sites in central and northern Komodo.

tags.name: Crinoid Wall
tags.description: A colorful wall in a small cove near the southern entrance to Loh Dasami at Nusa Kode, almost directly opposite Yellow Wall. Crevices hold sleeping slipper lobsters, cleaner shrimp, decorator crabs, red sea apples, and a great variety of tunicates and soft corals. An excellent slow-paced macro dive best done in the morning.

tags.name: Yellow Wall
tags.description: A dramatic wall along the east coast of Nusa Kode named for the profusion of bright yellow soft corals (Tubastrea) and robust sea cucumbers. Crevices harbor nudibranchs and ladybug amphipods, with clown triggerfish, fairy basslets, and occasional sharks and mantas cruising past. Best dived in the afternoon for optimal light. Medium to strong currents.

name: Cannibal Rock
validation_source: diveconcepts.com, divehappy.com, divekomodo.com, ScubaBoard user Dan (GPS)

tags.name: Cannibal Rock
tags.description: A small seamount in Loh Dasami Bay at Nusa Kode, carpeted in dense soft corals, crinoids, and sponges. Widely regarded as one of Indonesia's premier macro and critter diving sites. Home to an extraordinary density of invertebrate life including nudibranchs, sea apples, and crinoid shrimp, with occasional manta rays and reef sharks passing by.

tags.name: Boulders
tags.description: Large coral-encrusted boulders on the southwestern tip of Nusa Kode creating habitat for giant potato cod, Malabar groupers, bamboo sharks, and schools of giant trevally and fusiliers. Coleman shrimp on fire urchins and rare paddleflap scorpionfish (Rhinopia) attract macro photographers. Also a popular night dive.

name: Torpedo Alley
validation_source: lotusliveaboards.com, divessi.com, divephotoguide.com, ScubaBoard user Dan (GPS)

tags.name: Torpedo Alley
tags.description: A black sand slope in Horseshoe Bay at Nusa Kode and Komodo's most celebrated night dive. Named for the torpedo rays (electric rays) that blend into the substrate. After dark the slope comes alive with ambon scorpionfish, frogfish, stargazers, snake eels, cuttlefish, and abundant nudibranchs. Equally rewarding during the day for muck diving enthusiasts.

name: Manta Alley
validation_source: divehappy.com, divezone.net, divessi.com, ScubaBoard user Dan (GPS)

tags.name: Manta Alley
tags.description: A rocky channel at the far southern tip of Komodo Island where manta rays aggregate at cleaning stations. Peak season: sources conflict — divehappy.com and Dragon Dive Komodo report peak September–January; the original description cited November–March. Sightings of multiple mantas on a single dive are common in peak season. Strong currents and cold thermoclines (22–23°C) make this a challenging dive. Shallow cleaning stations at 5–15m; deeper sandy channels to ~20–25m. Maximum depth 40m. Also home to giant trevallies, sweetlips, and occasional mobula rays.
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-08: Seasonal peak conflict found. divehappy.com and dragondivekomodo.com both report September-January as peak; original Nov-March claim flagged as unconfirmed. Description updated to note conflict.
